name: Bas-Congo
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Consolidated in 1910 from the four former districts of Banana, Boma, Matadi, and Cataractes. Located at the mouth of the Congo River.

name: Moyen-Congo
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Middle Congo district. Later merged into Bas-Congo (south) and Lac Léopold II (north) by 1926.

name: Lac Léopold II
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Named after the lake (now Lake Mai-Ndombe). Located in the central-western Congo basin.

name: Équateur
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Named for its position straddling the equator. Later split into a smaller Équateur and Lulonga in 1912.

name: Bangala
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Named after the Bangala people along the Congo River. Located in the north-central part of the colony.

name: Ubangi
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Named after the Ubangi River, the largest right-bank tributary of the Congo. Bordered French Equatorial Africa.

name: Uele
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Named after the Uele River. Later split into Bas-Uele and Haut-Uele in 1912.

name: Aruwimi
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Named after the Aruwimi River. Part of the dense equatorial forest region explored by Henry Morton Stanley.

name: Stanleyville
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Named after Stanleyville (now Kisangani). Later split into a smaller Stanleyville, Lowa, Ituri, Maniema, and Kivu in 1912.

name: Katanga
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Made a vice-government general in 1910 from parts of the Stanley Falls and Lualaba districts. Rich in copper and cobalt — the economic heart of the colony. Later divided into Lomami, Tanganika-Moero, Haut-Luapula, and Lulua in 1912.

name: Kasai
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Named after the Kasai River. Later split into Sankuru (north) and a smaller Kasai (south) in 1912.

name: Kwango
era: Congo Free State / Early Belgian Congo
notes: Named after the Kwango River. Located in the southwestern part of the colony, bordering Portuguese Angola.
